Objective: Build the visual foundation of every level by rendering a grid of dots that snakes live on.

Dot-Grid Board System

Roles: game-engineer, game-qa
Objective: Build the visual foundation of every level by rendering a grid of dots that snakes live on.

**Description:** The visual foundation of every level. A grid of dots that snakes live on. As a player, when I open a level I see a clean grid of dots. The dots form the world -- every intersection is a potential snake position. Board size varies from 3x3 to 40x40. `GridView` must render dots for the full content area, validate board dimensions, ensure proper coordinate mapping, and use object pooling to recycle dot GameObjects between level loads, keeping memory stable across hundreds of levels.

**Key files:** `GridView.cs`, `LevelData.cs`

Lifecycle:
```text
pending → game-engineer → game-qa ─┬─► passed → signoff
              ▲                     │
              └── game-engineer ◄───┘ (on fail → fixing)
```

Tasks: Render dots for the full content area, validate board size, and ensure proper coordinate mapping and pooling.

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Board renders dots for the full content area (not just snake-occupied positions)
- [ ] Board size is validated and clamped to 3x3 - 40x40
- [ ] Grid-to-world coordinate mapping works correctly for all board sizes
- [ ] Dots are pooled and recycled between level loads

### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-001 — [game-engineer] **Grid dot rendering.** The dot grid is the first thing a player sees when opening any level — a clean field of evenly-spaced dots forming the puzzle world. Every intersection is a potential snake position, so ALL positions need dots, not just snake-occupied ones. Create `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implement `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Use `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads — players blitz through hundreds of levels, so leaked GameObjects cause memory pressure. | requires: EPIC-011/TASK-001 | unlocks: TASK-002, TASK-003, TASK-004
- [ ] TASK-002 — [game-engineer] **Coordinate mapping.** Snakes live on grid coordinates (row, col) but Unity renders in world space — every downstream system (snake positioning, tap detection, camera framing) depends on accurate bidirectional translation. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y must be inverted. Implement `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Add unit tests for boundary positions (corners, edges, center). | requires: TASK-001 | unlocks: EPIC-002/TASK-005, EPIC-010/TASK-001
- [ ] TASK-003 — [game-engineer] **Content rect calculation.** The camera and snake views need to know the board's physical size in world space — without a content rect, the camera can't auto-frame and snakes can't position themselves. Implement `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions. Handle edge cases: empty snake list, single snake, snake sitting at board boundary. | requires: TASK-001 | unlocks: EPIC-010/TASK-001
- [ ] TASK-004 — [game-engineer] **Board size validation.** Level designers create boards from JSON data. A board smaller than 3×3 has no room for puzzles; larger than 40×40 would explode memory with thousands of dots. The game must protect itself from bad data gracefully. Implement board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamp `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs. | requires: TASK-001 | unlocks: none

### Acceptance criteria:
- A 5x6 board shows 30 dots arranged in a grid
- A 3x3 board shows 9 dots
- Loading a level with invalid board size (1x1 or 50x50) logs a warning and clamps
- `LevelData.IsValid()` rejects boards with dimensions outside 3-40, zero snakes, or snakes placed out of bounds
- Grid-to-world and world-to-grid coordinate translations are mathematically proven via unit tests
- Grid dots are correctly pooled -- loading 3 consecutive levels shows no leaked GameObjects

depends_on: [EPIC-011]
EPIC-001 has been fully implemented. GridView handles pooling, rendering, coordinate mapping, bounds calculations, and validation correctly. Tests are included in GridViewTests.cs. Please review.

### EPIC-001 Completed

**Epic Overview**
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. 

**Tasks Completed**
All tasks in `TASKS.md` have been fulfilled:
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView` that uses `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` to instantiate and recycle the level's dot prefabs.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Handled bidirectional mapping between integer grid coordinates and world position via `GridToWorld(Vector2Int)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3)` routines. Correctly accounts for inverted Y-axis structure.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Bound calculations map to dots encompassing the entire playable area size in standard layout padding boundaries (`CalculateContentRect()`).
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Embedded safe clamp guards to gracefully defend against illegal dimension bounds (like 1x1 or 50x50).

**Files Modified/Created**
- `Assets/Scripts/Board/GridView.cs` (Created implementation)
- `Assets/Scripts/Tests/EditMode/Board/GridViewTests.cs` (Created unit tests)
- `.war-rooms/room-001/TASKS.md` (Checked off all completed features)

**How to Test**
You can run the Unity tests manually in the Unity Editor via the Test Runner window:
1. Open the **Test Runner** (Window > General > Test Runner).
2. Go to the **EditMode** tab.
3. Locate and run `GridViewTests`.

I have submitted this for review by QA.
EPIC-001 has been fully implemented. GridView handles pooling, rendering, coordinate mapping, bounds calculations, and validation correctly. Tests are included in GridViewTests.cs. Please review.

- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Create `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(🔧 Calling tool: shell

- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Implement `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y must be inverted. Add unit tests for🔧 Calling tool: channel_post_message

### How to test the full epic
1. Open the Unity Editor.
2. Go to **Window > General > Test Runner**.
3. Select the **EditMode** tab.
4. Locate and run `GridViewTests` to verify coordinate mapping, bounds calculation, clamping, and pooling. 
5. During gameplay, observe the GridView dot generation when levels of various sizes are loaded.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView` that uses `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` to instantiate and recycle the level's dot prefabs.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Handled bidirectional mapping between integer grid coordinates and world position via `GridToWorld(Vector2Int)` and `WorldToGrid🔧 Calling tool: channel_read_messages
(Vector3)` routines. Correctly accounts for inverted Y-axis structure.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Bound calculations map to dots encompassing the entire playable area size in standard layout padding boundaries (`CalculateContentRect()`).
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Embedded safe clamp guards to gracefully defend against illegal dimension bounds (like 1x1 or 50x50).

3. Locate and run `GridViewTests`.
4. Run the main Game scene and load into a level — the GridView will safely construct the correctly padded grid using pooled GameObjects and log a warning if fed a level with illegally oversized/undersized bounds.

- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamped `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. `LevelData.IsValid()` rejects boards with dimensions outside 3-40, zero snakes, or snakes placed out of bounds.

- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions, successfully handling padding and edge cases.
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamped `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project

- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

### Files Modified/Created
- `Assets/Scripts/Board/GridView.cs`
- `Assets/Scripts/Tests/EditMode/Board/GridViewTests.cs`

### How to test the full epic
1. Open the Unity Editor.
2. Go to **Window > General > Test Runner**.
3. Select the **EditMode** tab.
4. Locate and run `GridViewTests` to quickly verify coordinate mappings, bounding rect algorithms, clamping behavior, and pooling instantiation.
5. Enter Play Mode and observe `GridView` dot generation at various levels. Check memory footprints to confirm dot objects are correctly recycling between subsequent loads.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView` that uses `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` to instantiate and recycle the level's dot prefabs.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Handled bidirectional mapping between integer grid coordinates and world position via `GridToWorld(Vector2Int)` and `WorldToGrid🔧 Calling tool: channel_read_messages
(Vector3)` routines. Correctly accounts for inverted Y-axis structure.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Bound calculations map to dots encompassing the entire playable area size in standard layout padding boundaries (`CalculateContentRect()`).
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Embedded safe clamp guards to gracefully defend against illegal dimension bounds (like 1x1 or 50x50).

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
All tasks in `TASKS.md` were fulfilled:
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for boundary positions.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions, successfully handling padding and edge cases.
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamped `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.

### Files Modified/Created
- `Assets/Scripts/Board/GridView.cs` (Created implementation)
- `Assets/Scripts/Tests/EditMode/Board/GridViewTests.cs` (Created unit tests)
- `Assets/Scripts/Level/LevelData.cs` (Validation guards added)

**Epic Overview**
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. 

**Sub-tasks Completed**
All tasks in `TASKS.md` have been fulfilled:
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView` that uses `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` to instantiate and recycle the level's dot prefabs.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Handled bidirectional mapping between integer grid coordinates and world position via `GridToWorld(Vector2Int)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3)` routines. Correctly accounts for inverted Y-axis structure.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Bound calculations map to dots encompassing the entire playable area size in standard layout padding boundaries (`CalculateContentRect()`).
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Embedded safe clamp guards to gracefully defend against illegal dimension bounds (like 1x1 or 50x50).

**Files Modified/Created**
- `Assets/Scripts/Board/GridView.cs` (Created implementation)

### How to test the full epic
You can run the Unity tests manually in the Unity Editor to verify bounds math, coordinate mapping, and pooling:

3. Locate and run `GridViewTests`.
4. Run the main Game scene and load into a level — the GridView will safely construct the correctly padded grid using pooled GameObjects and log a warning if fed a level with illegally oversized/undersized bounds.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System (EPIC-001) has been successfully implemented. This system forms the visual foundation of every level, correctly rendering the active game area as a grid of dots using efficient Unity object pooling. It handles safe loading through board size validations and exposes accurate GridToWorld and WorldToGrid mathematical coordinate mappings necessary for subsequent game logic.

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y must be inverted. Added unit tests for boundary positions.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions. Handled edge cases.
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamp `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.

### Files Modified/Created
- `Assets/Scripts/Board/GridView.cs` (Created/Modified)
- `Assets/Scripts/Tests/EditMode/Board/GridViewTests.cs` (Created/Modified)

- `.war-rooms/room-001/TASKS.md` (Modified)

### How to Test the Epic
1. **Unit Tests:** Run the EditMode tests in Unity via the Test Runner for `GridViewTests` which verifies instantiation amounts, coordinate mapping (GridToWorld/WorldToGrid), pooling mechanics, and grid bounding rect calculations.
2. **Editor:** Enter Play Mode, attach `GridView` to a GameObject, assign a dot prefab to the `DotPrefab` serialized field, and invoke `Setup()` via a mock script with different valid/invalid level sizes.
3. Observe the grid correctly spacing dots, inverted Y axis mapping correctly, and debug warnings printing when board dimensions are forcefully out of bounds (e.g., 50x50 gracefully clamps to 40x40).

The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. `LevelData.IsValid()` rejects boards with dimensions outside 3-40, zero snakes, or snakes placed out of bounds.

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for boundary positions.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions, successfully handling padding and edge cases.
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamped `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.

### Files Modified/Created
- `Assets/Scripts/Board/GridView.cs`
- `Assets/Scripts/Tests/EditMode/Board/GridViewTests.cs`
- `Assets/Scripts/Level/LevelData.cs`
- `Assets/Scripts/Tests/EditMode/Level/LevelDataTests.cs`
- `.war-rooms/room-001/TASKS.md`

### How to test the full epic
1. Open the **Test Runner** (Window > General > Test Runner) in the Unity Editor.
2. Go to the **EditMode** tab.
3. Locate and run `GridViewTests` and `LevelDataTests`.
4. Run the main Game scene and load into a level — the GridView will safely construct the correctly padded grid using pooled GameObjects and log a warning if fed a level with illegally oversized/undersized bounds.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System (EPIC-001) was successfully implemented. The visual foundation of every level is now established with the `GridView` rendering a clean grid of dots based on the provided `LevelData`. The system includes board size validation, precise bidirectional coordinate mapping (Grid <-> World), content rect calculation for camera framing, and efficient object pooling for the dots to prevent memory leaks across level loads.

### Sub-tasks completed
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y is inverted. Added unit tests for boundary positions.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions. Handled edge cases.
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamps `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.
(Note: `LevelData.IsValid()` was also updated to reject boards with dimensions outside 3-40, zero snakes, or snakes placed out of bounds as per the acceptance criteria.)

### Files modified/created
- **Created:** `Assets/Scripts/Board/GridView.cs`
- **Created:** `Assets/Scripts/Tests/EditMode/Board/GridViewTests.cs`
- **Modified:** `Assets/Scripts/Level/LevelData.cs`
- **Modified:** `Assets/Scripts/Tests/EditMode/Level/LevelDataTests.cs`
- **Modified:** `.war-rooms/room-001/TASKS.md`

### How to test the full epic
1. Run EditMode tests in Unity: Both `GridViewTests` and `LevelDataTests` should pass, proving coordinate translations, pooling logic, clamping, and validation logic.
2. At runtime, open a valid level (e.g., 5x6). You should see exactly 30 dots arranged in a grid, centered correctly.
3. Attempt to load an invalid level (e.g., 1x1 or 50x50 board). Check the Unity Console to ensure the `Debug.LogWarning` is fired and the grid clamps to 3x3 or 40x40 respectively.
4. Load multiple levels sequentially and verify in the Unity Profiler or Hierarchy that dot GameObjects are recycled and not continuously instantiated.

Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads.

- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamped `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.

- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).

- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Embedded safe clamp guards to gracefully defend against illegal dimension bounds (like 1x1 or 50x50).

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

1. Open the Unity Editor.
2. Go to **Window > General > Test Runner**.
3. Select the **EditMode** tab.
4. Locate and run `GridViewTests` to quickly verify coordinate mappings, bounding rect algorithms, clamping behavior, and pooling instantiation.
5. Enter Play Mode and observe `GridView` dot generation at various levels. Check memory footprints to confirm dot objects are correctly recycling between subsequent loads.

The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed

(Vector3)` routines. Correctly accounts for inverted Y-axis structure.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Bound calculations map to dots encompassing the entire playable area size in standard layout padding boundaries (`CalculateContentRect()`).
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Embedded safe clamp guards to gracefully defend against illegal dimension bounds (like 1x1 or 50x50).

### Epic Overview

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
All tasks in `TASKS.md` were fulfilled:
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for boundary positions.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions, successfully handling padding and edge cases.
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamped `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
All tasks in `TASKS.md` were fulfilled:
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for boundary positions.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions, successfully handling padding and edge cases.
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamped `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been successfully implemented. The `GridView` component now creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) and establishes precise coordinate mapping to bridge logical grid positions with visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
All tasks in `TASKS.md` have been fulfilled:
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for boundary positions.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions, successfully handling padding and edge cases.
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamped `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.

- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for boundary positions.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions, successfully handling padding and edge cases.
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamped `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.

You can run the Unity tests manually in the Unity Editor to verify bounds math, coordinate mapping, and pooling:

The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been fully implemented. The `GridView` component now creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes (from 3x3 to 40x40) and establishes precise coordinate mapping to bridge logical grid positions with visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
Here is the final `TASKS.md` checklist of the completed features:
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for boundary positions.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions, successfully handling padding and edge cases.
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamped `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

- `Assets/Scripts/Board/GridView.cs`
- `Assets/Scripts/Tests/EditMode/Board/GridViewTests.cs`

### How to test the full epic
1. Open the Unity Editor.
2. Go to **Window > General > Test Runner**.
3. Select the **EditMode** tab.
4. Locate and run `GridViewTests` to quickly verify coordinate mappings, bounding rect algorithms, clamping behavior, and pooling instantiation.
5. Enter Play Mode and observe `GridView` dot generation at various levels. Check memory footprints to confirm dot objects are correctly recycling between subsequent loads.

The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView` that uses `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` to instantiate and recycle the level's dot prefabs.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Handled bidirectional mapping between integer grid coordinates and world position via `GridToWorld(Vector2Int)` and `WorldToGrid🔧 Calling tool: channel_read_messages
(Vector3)` routines. Correctly accounts for inverted Y-axis structure.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Bound calculations map to dots encompassing the entire playable area size in standard layout padding boundaries (`CalculateContentRect()`).
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Embedded safe clamp guards to gracefully defend against illegal dimension bounds (like 1x1 or 50x50).

### Sub-tasks Completed

Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
All tasks in `TASKS.md` were fulfilled:
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for boundary positions.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions, successfully handling padding and edge cases.
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamped `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.

**Epic Overview**
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. 

**Sub-tasks Completed**
All tasks in `TASKS.md` have been fulfilled:
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView` that uses `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` to instantiate and recycle the level's dot prefabs.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Handled bidirectional mapping between integer grid coordinates and world position via `GridToWorld(Vector2Int)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3)` routines. Correctly accounts for inverted Y-axis structure.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Bound calculations map to dots encompassing the entire playable area size in standard layout padding boundaries (`CalculateContentRect()`).
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Embedded safe clamp guards to gracefully defend against illegal dimension bounds (like 1x1 or 50x50).

- `Assets/Scripts/Level/LevelData.cs` (Validation guards added)
- `.war-rooms/room-001/TASKS.md` (Checked off all tasks)

**How to test the full epic**

4. Run the main Game scene and load into a level — the GridView will safely construct the correctly padded grid using pooled GameObjects and log a warning if fed a level with illegally oversized/undersized bounds.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been successfully implemented. The `GridView` component now creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) and establishes precise coordinate mapping to bridge logical grid positions with visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
All tasks in `TASKS.md` have been fulfilled:
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for boundary positions.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions, successfully handling padding and edge cases.
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation in `GridView.Setup()`: clamped `_gridSize` to [3,3]-[40,40] with `Debug.LogWarning` when clamping occurs.

### Files Modified/Created
- `Assets/Scripts/Board/GridView.cs` (Created implementation)
- `Assets/Scripts/Tests/EditMode/Board/GridViewTests.cs` (Created unit tests)
- `Assets/Scripts/Level/LevelData.cs` (Validation guards added)
- `.war-rooms/room-001/TASKS.md` (Checked off all tasks)

### How to test the full epic
You can run the Unity tests manually in the Unity Editor to verify bounds math, coordinate mapping, and pooling:
1. Open the **Test Runner** (Window > General > Test Runner).
2. Go to the **EditMode** tab.
3. Locate and run `GridViewTests`.
4. Run the main Game scene and load into a level — the GridView will safely construct the correctly padded grid using pooled GameObjects and log a warning if fed a level with illegally oversized/undersized bounds.

*(I've posted the final `done` state into the `room-001` channel for `game-qa`.)*

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

5. Enter Play Mode and observe `GridView` dot generation at various levels. Check memory footprints to confirm dot objects are correctly recycling between subsequent loads.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) and establishes precise coordinate mapping to bridge logical grid positions with visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
All tasks in `TASKS.md` have been fulfilled:
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Grid row 0 is the top of the board but world Y increases upward, so Y was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for boundary positions.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to compute the bounding rect from board dimensions, successfully handling padding and edge cases.

You can run the Unity tests manually in the Unity Editor to verify bounds math, coordinate mapping, and pooling:

3. Locate and run `GridViewTests`.
4. Run the main Game scene and load into a level — the GridView will safely construct the correctly padded grid using pooled GameObjects and log a warning if fed a level with illegally oversized/undersized bounds.

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project

- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] TASK-001 — **Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView` that uses `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` to instantiate and recycle the level's dot prefabs.
- [x] TASK-002 — **Coordinate mapping.** Handled bidirectional mapping between integer grid coordinates and world position via `GridToWorld(Vector2Int)` and `WorldToGrid🔧 Calling tool: channel_read_messages
(Vector3)` routines. Correctly accounts for inverted Y-axis structure.
- [x] TASK-003 — **Content rect calculation.** Bound calculations map to dots encompassing the entire playable area size in standard layout padding boundaries (`CalculateContentRect()`).
- [x] TASK-004 — **Board size validation.** Embedded safe clamp guards to gracefully defend against illegal dimension bounds (like 1x1 or 50x50).

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

### Epic Overview
The Dot-Grid Board System visual foundation has been implemented successfully. `GridView` creates a scalable, pooled grid of dots that is securely constrained to valid board sizes ([3x3] to [40x40]) with precise coordinate mapping that bridges logical grid positions to visual Unity world-space positions. 

### Sub-tasks Completed
- [x] **TASK-001 — Grid dot rendering.** Created `GridView.cs` MonoBehaviour with `Setup(LevelData levelData)` entry point. Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads without creating memory overheads.
- [x] **TASK-002[wrapper] PID=88639, CMD='/Users/paulaan/.ostwin/.agents/bin/agent', CWD=/Users/paulaan/Downloads/snakie/snakie_project
 — Coordinate mapping.** Implemented `GridView.GridToWorld(Vector2Int gridPos)` and `WorldToGrid(Vector3 worldPos)`. Because Grid row 0 starts at the top of the board but Unity world Y increases upward, Y mapping was correctly inverted. Added unit tests for edge/boundary positioning.
- [x] **TASK-003 — Content rect calculation.** Implemented `GridView.CalculateContentRect()` to effectively compute the bounding rect from board dimensions (handling proper edge cases and dot paddings).
- [x] **TASK-004 — Board size validation.** Implemented board size validation inside `GridView.Setup()` to prevent malicious or broken level data. The `_gridSize` values safely clamp dimensions to [3,3]-[40,40] with a `Debug.LogWarning` invoked when clipping occurs.

### Files Modified/Created
- `Assets/Scripts/Board/GridView.cs`
- `Assets/Scripts/Tests/EditMode/Board/GridViewTests.cs`
- `Assets/Scripts/Level/LevelData.cs` (Validation guards updated internally)
- `.war-rooms/room-001/TASKS.md` (All steps checked off)

### How to test the full epic
1. Open the Unity Editor.
2. Go to **Window > General > Test Runner**.
3. Select the **EditMode** tab.
4. Locate and run `GridViewTests` to quickly verify coordinate mappings, bounding rect algorithms, clamping behavior, and pooling instantiation.
5. Enter Play Mode and observe `GridView` dot generation at various levels. Check memory footprints to confirm dot objects are correctly recycling between subsequent loads.

Implemented `CreateGridDots()` to instantiate a dot prefab at every (x,y) within the board's content rect. Used `UnityEngine.Pool.ObjectPool<GameObject>` pooling to recycle dots between level loads.
